items.title: Michele Magrini
items.subtext2: MSc in Applied Mathematics for AI

items.title: MSc in Applied Mathematics (AI)
items.subtext2: Building advanced expertise in AI with a strong mathematical foundation. Focused on deep learning, generative models, and NLP, supported by rigorous training in statistical inference and mathematical modeling. Applying these methods to complex systems, physics-informed learning, and emerging areas such as quantum AI.

items.title: BSc in Mathematics for AI
items.subtext2: Built a strong foundation in artificial intelligence through coursework in machine learning, data analysis, and mathematical modeling. Applied theoretical knowledge in projects involving optimization algorithms, algorithmic decision-making, and database systems.

items.title: HS Diploma in Computer Science
items.subtext2: Foundational experience in electronics, telecommunications, networking, and software development (front-end and back-end).

items.title: Machine Learning Researcher
items.subtext2: Design and optimization of machine learning models and algorithms for embedded, low-power, and resource-constrained environments.

items.title: XAI Analysis of Foreshocks and Aftershocks in Swiss sequence
items.subtext2: Analysis of SHAP on a Swiss microseismic sequence and explore new XAI techniques.

items.title: Norcia 2016 Station-Level and Network-Wide SHAP Explanation
items.subtext2: Analysis of SHAP results for the understanding of Seismic Cycle.

items.title: Explainable CNN for Earthquake Phase Classification
items.subtext2: RGB spectrogram CNN with SHAP can reveal time-frequency features linked to fault-zone evolution.
